sections.paragraph:
ὀκνέω 1 aor. ὤκνησα (s. next entry; Hom.+; pap, LXX) to hold back from doing someth., hesitate, delay w. inf. foll. (Diod S 10, 33, 1 ὀκ. ἀποθνῄσκειν; Appian, Mithrid. 57 §230; Oenomaus in Eus., PE 5, 21, 2; Just., D. 142, 1; Tat 23, 1) μὴ ὀκνήσῃς διελθεῖν ἕως ἡμῶν come over to us without delay Ac 9:38 (sim. Num 22:16 μὴ ὀκνήσῃς ἐλθεῖν πρός με. Field, p. 118: ‘a courteous mode of pressing a request’; Sir 7:35; cp. Lucian, Necyom. 11 μὴ ὀκνήσῃς εἰπεῖν; POxy 1769, 7; Philo, Aet. M. 84; Jos., Vi. 251, C. Ap. 1, 15; Field, Notes 118). οὐκ ὀκνήσω … συγκατατάξαι ταῖς ἑρμηνείαις I will not hesitate to include in my expositions Papias (2:3).—DELG s.v. 1 ὄκνο. M-M. Spicq.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

someth. someth. = something

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Oenomaus Oenomaus , II a.d.—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

sim. sim. = similarly

Field Field = FF.—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Field, Notes Field, Notes = Notes on the Translation of the NT

Papias Papias , early Christian writer, II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 702
